label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the options is wrong based on the news article provided. Let me read through the options and the article again to make sure. First, the options are A, B, C, D. The article says that President Bush mentioned that America's space program will go on. So option A says that they will give up the space program because of the accident. That seems incorrect because the article states the opposite. The president said the space program will continue. So A is likely the wrong one. Option B: NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster. The article states, "NASA launched an investigation into the disaster and began searching for the astronauts' remains. It said that although there had been some data failures it was too early to nail down a precise cause." So B is correct because it's stated that it was too early to determine the cause. So B is a correct statement according to the article. Option C: Before the Columbia disaster, no shuttles had exploded in the course of landing. The article mentions that "In 42 years of US' human space flight, there had never been an accident in the descent to Earth or landing. Challenger exploded just after take-off." So Columbia was the first to have an accident during descent/landing. So C is correct. Option D: Take-off and re-entry into Earth's atmosphere are the most dangerous parts of a space mission. The article states, "Take-off and re-entry into Earth's atmosphere are the most dangerous parts of a space mission." So D
